items.correct: Energy used to maintain basic bodily functions at complete rest.
items.why: Students think BMR is about exercise. It's the baseline energy needed just to STAY ALIVE - breathing, heart, brain, organ function - typically 60-70% of total daily energy use even before any activity.

items.q: Why are dried foods less prone to spoilage?
items.correct: Low water activity prevents microorganisms from growing.
items.misconception: Bacteria do not like dry textures.
items.why: Students think it's about texture preference. Microbes need available water. Drying reduces WATER ACTIVITY below the threshold microbes can use - no water = no metabolism = no spoilage.

items.correct: Proteins denaturing and bonding to form a solid structure when heated.
items.misconception: The egg shell breaking when boiled.
items.why: Students think coagulation is something physical happening to the shell. It's PROTEIN CHEMISTRY: heat unfolds the proteins, which then bond together, turning liquid egg into solid - what makes a fried egg set.

items.q: How long is exclusive breastfeeding recommended?
items.why: It's 6 months. “3 months” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which nutrient is especially recommended pre-conception?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: folate. “vitamin K” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The real figure is 400 µg. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
